verification_methodology: Six-layer verification: (1) runtime testing with cache simulation framework (8-way set-associative LRU, 32KB L1, 256KB L2, 64B lines); (2) property-based testing of Work-Preservation Lemma (compositionality, work-bound preservation, structural induction base cases, specialization idempotence, adversarial work bound, work monotonicity — 2,700+ trials, 100% pass); (3) semantic preservation verification of IR repair transformations via simulation; (4) post-compilation translation validation; (5) compositional verification testing; (6) scalability evaluation at realistic input sizes (up to n=1,048,576) with real parallel baseline comparisons (rayon) and theory-practice gap analysis. All 1,516 tests pass.

claims.claim: Hash-Partition Locality Theorem: Q ≤ c₃(pT/B + T) with c₃ ≤ 4 in the cache-resident regime (n/B ≤ M/B).
claims.verification: Paper proof using SSS bounded-independence concentration. Three regimes characterized: cache-resident (bound ratio 0.03), transitional (~1.0), capacity-dominated (~1.9).
claims.limitation: SSS moment-method bound with k=8 is conservative (~2x gap). Bound holds tightly only in cache-resident regime. Not mechanically verified.

claims.claim: Average cache bound ratio of 0.49 at sizes up to n=65,536 (cache-resident regime).
claims.limitation: Uses simulated cache (8-way set-associative LRU), not hardware performance counters.

claims.claim: At large scale (n=10^6), cache bound ratio rises to 0.80 as capacity misses dominate.
claims.limitation: Cache simulation at scale; traces truncated to 10^6 elements for tractability.

claims.claim: HP compilation is 2.85x slower (geometric mean) than rayon parallel library across 7 workloads.
claims.limitation: Rayon uses 10 threads; ratio depends on hardware thread count.

claims.claim: O(pT) work with c₁ ≤ 4 via staged partial evaluation (Work-Preservation Lemma).
claims.verification: Paper proof sketch validated by 2,700+ property-based tests across 6 properties (100% pass).
claims.limitation: Not mechanized in a proof assistant (Coq, Lean, Isabelle). Proof sketch has structural induction but loop bounds estimated conservatively.

claims.claim: 51/51 algorithms (100%) meet 2x cache-miss target at sizes ≤ 16,384 (cache-resident). 75% meet target at sizes up to 65,536.
claims.limitation: 75% pass rate at n=65,536 reflects capacity-miss dominance, not hash-partition failure.

claims.claim: CRCW-Arbitrary is implemented as deterministic lowest-index-writer (Priority) resolution.
claims.limitation: Sound for cache-miss bounds (address-set dependent) but does not capture full non-deterministic semantics.

claims.claim: SSS bound overshoots empirical overflow by ~2x for structured PRAM access patterns.
Scaling exponent of hash-partition workload is 0.96 (near-linear).

known_limitations: Work-Preservation Lemma not mechanized in a proof assistant
Cache simulation uses software LRU model, not hardware performance counters
SSS bound with k=8 is conservative (~2x gap) for structured access patterns
Theorem bound holds tightly only in cache-resident regime (n/B ≤ M/B)
HP compilation is 2.85x slower than rayon parallel library (geometric mean)
HP achieves 0.01-0.26x speedup vs hand-optimized sequential baselines
